feedback.text: Hi, Mir’s is not only about video games that stop and shut down, but also about property rights. It is a shame that we only buy ‘licences’ on online platforms and that we no longer own the games. I ask you to do this kind of business model if we buy a game at the selling price (up to 60, and who want to increase the full price at release now?!), then we should also have this game for consumers. The current model is: for example, Steam gives us the right to play the games they distribute on their platform, but if they have loud, they can take me back at any time. But we don’t live in a dystopia, or not? Good Old Games has always done this differently, actually buying games there and even trying to protect games as cultural goods. Everyone should take GOG as an example. To be honest, I don’t know how such practices are allowed in the EU. I would ask you to take action against it. Yours sincerely, Cihangir Gürbüz EU Citizens Germany
